channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Kwara judiciary sacks two registrars, demotes magistrate

item.description:
The Kwara State Judicial Service Commission has dismissed two court registrars and demoted a magistrate over gross misconduct, reaffirming its commitment t

item.title: Police arrest father in Ondo for burning daughter’s private part
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Ondo State Police have arrested a father for allegedly burning his 10-year-old daughter’s private part over bedwetting. Read more on this child abuse case.

Two men, Haruna Mamuda and Sadiq Umar, have been convicted by an FCT High Court for EFCC impersonation and defrauding the public. Sentencing is set for Jul

item.title: Illicit arms moving from North to South-West, says IG
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
IG Tunji Disu raises alarm over illicit arms moving from Northern Nigeria to the South-West, warning of growing security threats.

item.title: ‘227,’ ‘Sanford And Son’ Star Hal Williams Dies At 91
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Veteran American actor Hal Williams, best known for his memorable roles in the hit sitcoms 227 and Sanford and Son, has died at the age of 91. Williams died on July 15 at his home in Rancho Mirage, California. His representative confirmed his passing, while reports said the actor died of natural cau

item.title: UN finds looted gold, gum arabic bankrolling Sudan war
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Proceeds from looted gold and gum arabic are being used by warring parties in Sudan to fund the war, the UN human rights office has verified. A new OHCHR report described gum arabic and gold sales as “a major revenue source” for the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and paramilitary Rapid Supp

item.description:

Read More: https://punchng.com/bandits-kill-vigilante-leader-in-katsina/

item.title: US Limits Length Of Visas For Students, Journalists
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Under a change that could be implemented as soon as September, foreigners on student visas would be admitted for the length of their academic programme up to a maximum of four years. The post US Limits Length Of Visas For Students, Journalists appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Amaechi’s mother dies at 89
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The family said details of the funeral arrangements would be announced later. The post Amaechi’s mother dies at 89 appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Court orders parties to maintain status quo in Benue APC leadership suit
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Federal High Court in Abuja has ordered the All Progressives Congress (APC) and the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) to maintain the status quo in a dispute over the party’s candidates that emerged from the Benue State APC primaries for the 2027 general election. item.title: Navy foils attempted wellhead sabotage, arrests suspect in Bonny
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Nigerian Navy has thwarted an attempted act of crude oil theft targeting critical oil infrastructure in Bonny, Rivers State, reinforcing its commitment to safeguarding Nigeria's strategic oil and gas assets under Operation Delta Sentinel. The post Navy foils attempted wellhead sabotage, arrests

item.title: Guardiola names player he would’ve loved to coach

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Central Bank of Nigeria, CBN, has issued fresh guidelines on how licensed Bureau de Change, BDC, operators can purchase foreign exchange. The guidelines, signed by the CBN’s Director of the Trade and Exchange Department, Aderinola Shonekan, were issued in a circular dated July 15, 2026. Ac

item.description: Ogun State Governor, Dapo Abiodun, has approved the extension of the one-day-off-duty work palliative for eligible public and civil servants in the state. The Governor has also approved the payment of a ₦10,000 monthly transport allowance for an additional three months, covering July to September 20

item.description: Existing-home prices just hit a new record high — but there are plenty of newly built homes selling at a discount, as desperate builders cut prices.

item.description: President Bola Tinubu has launched three intervention programmes valued at about $3.05bn to tackle poverty, strengthen healthcare and education, support displaced persons, and boost livelihoods across Nigeria. item.title: Root delivers again for England to level ODI series against India
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Joe Root notched a stunning 99no to deliver again for his country as England secured a thrilling four-wicket win to level the one-day international series against India at Sophia Gardens in Cardiff.

item.description: Former Sweden striker, Zlatan Ibrahimovic, has revealed that former Real Madrid manager Zinedine Zidane must bring his management to the France national team when he becomes the head coach. item.title: Samsung’s 55-inch Frame art TV is $200 cheaper than usual
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Samsung’s Frame is different from your average 4K TV. Its biggest selling point involves what it does when you aren’t actively using it. item.title: Tinubu’s Assent to FMC Oleh Bill Ends Decades of Federal Neglect, Says IDU President
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: The President of the Isoko Development Union (IDU), Prof. Chris Akpotu, has described President Bola Tinubu’s assent to the bill establishing the Federal Medical Centre (FMC) in Oleh, Delta State,

item.title: Akpabio Threatens Contactors With Legal Action Over Faulty NASS Microphones
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Senate President Godswill Akpabio has threatened legal action against the contractor responsible for the renovation of the National Assembly complex following persistent technical faults affecting the microphones in both the Senate and House of Representatives chambers. Akpabio issued the warning du

item.title: White House confirms Trump will attend World Cup final
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The White House has confirmed that United States President Donald Trump will attend Sunday's 2026 FIFA World Cup final between Argentina and Spain at the New York-New Jersey Stadium. The post White House confirms Trump will attend World Cup final appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Sweetgreen Shares Slide to Three-Month Low on Cyclosporiasis Outbreak Fears
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Sweetgreen Inc. has lost more than a quarter of its market value in just four days as investors dump the stock due to fears about outbreak of cyclosporiasis — an intestinal infection caused by parasites in raw produce and water that has sickened thousands across the US.

item.title: PHOTOS: Jonathan’s son graduates from UK university
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Former President Goodluck Jonathan's son, Ariwera Jonathan has graduated from Manchester Metropolitan University in the United Kingdom. The post PHOTOS: Jonathan’s son graduates from UK university appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: World Cup 2026: I Am Disappointed In Myself –France Defender
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Aston Villa defender Lucas Digne has disclosed that he was disappointed in himself following France’s 2-0 loss to Spain in the FIFA World Cup 2026 semi-final. He was subbed off in the 72nd minute after a challenging match where he conceded a penalty and struggled defensively. Read Also:World C

item.description: Alex Enumah in Abuja A High Court of the Federal Capital Territory (FCT), in Apo, Abuja, on Thursday, ordered the final forfeiture of assets worth about N8.97 billion linked to

item.title: Founders Fund hires former OpenAI exec Ryan Beiermeister (and not because of her ‘Mafia’ skills)
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: Ryan Beiermeister, who demonstrated cool analysis in the Founders Fund YouTube series "Mafia," has joined the firm as a partner.
